title: Climate policy critique
input_text: The new climate policy feels like a one-size-fits-all hammer on every industry. Instead of letting businesses innovate, it slams them with rigid quotas that will only send jobs overseas.

title: Housing affordability
input_text: Our city needs more affordable housing, but the current plan prioritizes luxury towers that only line the pockets of developers. We should cap permit fees, expand transit-oriented development, and protect the renters already living in the neighborhood.

title: Tech privacy concerns
input_text: I keep seeing new privacy opt-ins that make it impossible for regular users to keep their data private. Companies should ship products that assume privacy by default, not trick people with dense consent walls.

title: Education reform
input_text: Standardized tests have turned classrooms into test prep factories. Let’s redirect those dollars toward smaller class sizes and meaningful project-based learning that actually prepares kids for the workplace.

title: Travel safety
input_text: Tourism boards keep telling us the city is safe, but I saw three new security checkpoints pop up overnight. We need transparent reporting on crime data and smarter lighting instead of catchy slogans.
